note: Rule summaries are original paraphrases for tooling purposes. Consult the SEI CERT C Coding Standard for normative wording.

ARR30-C.summary: Do not form or use out-of-bounds pointers or array subscripts.
ARR30-C.fix: Bound-check every index derived from input or computation.

ARR32-C.summary: Ensure size arguments for variable-length arrays are valid and bounded.
ARR32-C.fix: Avoid VLAs; use fixed maximum-size arrays.

ARR36-C.summary: Only subtract or relationally compare pointers into the same array.
ARR36-C.fix: Compare indices instead of pointers from different objects.

ARR37-C.summary: Do not do pointer arithmetic on a pointer to a non-array object.
ARR37-C.fix: A single object is an array of one; do not step past it.

ARR38-C.summary: Do not pass library functions arguments that make them form invalid pointers.
ARR38-C.fix: Validate the length against the real size of the buffer.

ARR39-C.summary: Do not add an already byte-scaled integer to a typed pointer.
ARR39-C.fix: Pointer arithmetic scales by element size; do not multiply by sizeof yourself.

CON30-C.summary: Release thread-specific storage before the thread ends.
CON30-C.fix: Register a destructor with tss_create, or free explicitly before returning.

CON31-C.summary: Do not destroy a mutex that is still held.
CON31-C.fix: Unlock the mutex, and ensure no thread can acquire it, before destroying.

CON32-C.summary: Synchronize access to bit-fields shared between threads.
CON32-C.fix: Adjacent bit-fields share a storage unit; guard the whole unit with one lock.

CON33-C.summary: Avoid race conditions from library functions that use static internal state.
CON33-C.fix: Use reentrant variants (strtok_r, localtime_r) in concurrent code.

CON34-C.summary: Give thread-shared objects a storage duration spanning every accessing thread.
CON34-C.fix: Use static or dynamically allocated storage, never a caller's stack frame.

CON35-C.summary: Acquire multiple locks in one global order to prevent deadlock.
CON35-C.fix: Document a lock ordering and take locks in that order everywhere.

CON36-C.summary: Loop around any wait that can wake spuriously.
CON36-C.fix: Wrap cnd_wait in a while loop that re-tests the predicate.

CON37-C.summary: Do not call signal() in a multithreaded program.
CON37-C.fix: Use the thread-aware signal APIs of your platform instead.

CON38-C.summary: Use condition variables so that both safety and liveness hold.
CON38-C.fix: Signal under the mutex, and use broadcast when several waiters may proceed.

CON39-C.summary: Do not join or detach a thread that was already joined or detached.
CON39-C.fix: Track each thread's state and join or detach it exactly once.

CON40-C.summary: Do not reference the same atomic object twice within one expression.
CON40-C.fix: Read the atomic once into a local, then operate on the local.

CON41-C.summary: Loop around calls that may fail spuriously.
CON41-C.fix: Retry compare-exchange in a loop until it succeeds or genuinely fails.

CON43-C.summary: Eliminate data races in multithreaded code.
CON43-C.fix: Protect every shared mutable object with a mutex or make it atomic.

DCL30-C.summary: Do not let object addresses escape their lifetime (e.g., returning pointers to locals).
DCL30-C.fix: Return by value, use caller-provided buffers, or static storage with documented semantics.

DCL31-C.summary: Declare every identifier before it is used.
DCL31-C.fix: Include the declaring header, or add a prototype above the first use.

DCL36-C.summary: Do not give one identifier contradictory linkage classifications.
DCL36-C.fix: Pick one linkage and apply it consistently to every declaration.

DCL37-C.summary: Do not declare or define reserved identifiers (leading underscore, standard names).

DCL38-C.summary: Use the standard syntax when declaring a flexible array member.
DCL38-C.fix: Declare it as `type name[];` as the last member, not `[0]` or `[1]`.

DCL39-C.summary: Do not let padding or uninitialized struct bytes cross a trust boundary.
DCL39-C.fix: Zero the whole structure before populating it, or copy field by field.

DCL40-C.summary: Do not declare the same function or object in two incompatible ways.
DCL40-C.fix: Declare each entity once in a header and include it everywhere.

DCL41-C.summary: Do not declare variables inside a switch body before the first case label.
DCL41-C.fix: Move the declaration above the switch, or into the clause that uses it.

DCL42-C.summary: Assert the unsequenced or reproducible attributes only on functions that truly have them.
DCL42-C.fix: Remove the attribute unless the function is genuinely side-effect free.

ENV30-C.summary: Do not modify the object referenced by a function's const-in-spirit return value.
ENV30-C.fix: Copy the data before altering it; the library owns that storage.

ENV31-C.summary: Do not reuse an environment pointer after an operation that may invalidate it.
ENV31-C.fix: Re-fetch the environment after any call that can modify it.

ENV32-C.summary: Make every exit handler return normally rather than terminating or jumping.
ENV32-C.fix: Do not call exit or longjmp from within an atexit handler.

ENV33-C.summary: Do not invoke command processors via system().
ENV33-C.fix: Call specific APIs (exec-family with fixed argv) or remove the shell-out entirely.

ENV34-C.summary: Do not retain pointers returned by functions whose buffers may be reused.
ENV34-C.fix: Copy the result into your own storage immediately.

ERR30-C.summary: Set errno to zero before the call, and test it only where the standard defines it.
ERR30-C.fix: Zero errno, call, then check errno only if the return value indicates failure.

ERR32-C.summary: Do not treat errno as meaningful when its value is indeterminate.
ERR32-C.fix: Only read errno after a call documented to set it.

ERR33-C.summary: Detect and handle standard library errors (check return values).
ERR33-C.fix: Check every fallible call's return value and propagate errors.

ERR34-C.summary: Detect errors when converting strings to numbers (atoi cannot report failure).
ERR34-C.fix: Use strtol/strtoul with endptr and errno checks.

EXP30-C.summary: Do not write code whose result depends on the order side effects are evaluated in.
EXP30-C.fix: Split the expression into separate statements with explicit sequencing.

EXP32-C.summary: Never access a volatile object through a non-volatile reference.
EXP32-C.fix: Keep the volatile qualifier on every pointer and alias to the object.

EXP33-C.fix: Initialize every object at its point of declaration.

EXP34-C.fix: Check pointers (including allocator returns) before every dereference.

EXP35-C.summary: Do not modify an object that has only temporary lifetime.
EXP35-C.fix: Copy the value into an object you own before modifying it.

EXP36-C.summary: Do not cast a pointer to a type with stricter alignment requirements.
EXP36-C.fix: Copy through a correctly-aligned object, or use memcpy.

EXP37-C.summary: Pass exactly the number and types of arguments a function expects.
EXP37-C.fix: Include the prototype so the compiler can check the call.

EXP39-C.summary: Do not access an object through a pointer of an incompatible type.
EXP39-C.fix: Use memcpy or properly typed interfaces instead of pointer type-punning.

EXP40-C.summary: Never modify an object declared const.
EXP40-C.fix: Remove the const, or stop writing through the cast-away pointer.

EXP42-C.summary: Do not include padding bytes when comparing structures.
EXP42-C.fix: Compare member by member rather than with memcmp on the whole struct.

EXP43-C.summary: Respect the aliasing contract of restrict-qualified pointers.
EXP43-C.fix: Do not let two restrict pointers reach the same object.

EXP44-C.summary: Do not rely on side effects inside sizeof, _Alignof or _Generic.
EXP44-C.fix: Hoist the expression out; those operands are not evaluated.

EXP45-C.summary: Do not perform assignments inside selection or iteration condition expressions.
EXP45-C.fix: Move the assignment out of the condition.

EXP46-C.summary: Do not use a Boolean-valued expression as an operand of a bitwise operator.
EXP46-C.fix: Use the logical operators && and || for Boolean logic.

EXP47-C.summary: Do not request a type from va_arg that does not match what was passed.
EXP47-C.fix: Match the promoted type of the argument exactly.

FIO30-C.summary: Never pass externally influenced data as a printf-family format string.
FIO30-C.fix: Use a literal format: printf("%s", user_data).

FIO32-C.summary: Do not apply file-only operations to a device.
FIO32-C.fix: Verify the path refers to a regular file before operating on it.

FIO34-C.summary: Distinguish characters read from a file from EOF/WEOF.
FIO34-C.fix: Store getc results in int, not char, before comparing to EOF.

FIO37-C.summary: Do not assume a successful fgets or fgetws yields a non-empty string.
FIO37-C.fix: Check the returned string's length before indexing it.

FIO38-C.summary: Do not copy a FILE object by value.
FIO38-C.fix: Pass and store FILE pointers, never FILE values.

FIO39-C.summary: Flush or reposition a stream when switching between reading and writing.
FIO39-C.fix: Call fflush or fseek between a read and a write on the same stream.

FIO40-C.summary: Reset the destination string when fgets or fgetws fails.
FIO40-C.fix: Set the buffer to a known state before using it after a failed read.

FIO41-C.summary: Do not pass a side-effecting stream expression to getc, putc, getwc or putwc.
FIO41-C.fix: Evaluate the stream expression into a variable first; these may be macros.

FIO42-C.summary: Close every file once it is no longer needed.
FIO42-C.fix: Pair each successful fopen with an fclose on every exit path.

FIO44-C.summary: Only pass fsetpos values that fgetpos produced.
FIO44-C.fix: Do not synthesise or arithmetic on fpos_t values.

FIO45-C.summary: Avoid time-of-check to time-of-use races on file access.
FIO45-C.fix: Open the file once and operate on the descriptor, not the path.

FIO46-C.summary: Do not use a FILE pointer after the stream is closed.
FIO46-C.fix: Set the pointer to NULL immediately after fclose.

FIO47-C.summary: Keep format strings well-formed and matched to their arguments.
FIO47-C.fix: Check every conversion specifier against the argument it consumes.

FLP30-C.summary: Do not drive a loop counter with a floating-point variable.
FLP30-C.fix: Count with an integer and derive the float value inside the loop.

FLP32-C.summary: Prevent or detect domain and range errors in floating-point math functions.
FLP32-C.fix: Validate math-function inputs and check errno/fetestexcept.

FLP34-C.summary: Confirm a floating-point value fits the destination before converting it.
FLP34-C.fix: Range-check against the destination type's limits first.

FLP36-C.summary: Watch for precision loss when converting integers to floating-point.
FLP36-C.fix: Use a wider float type, or keep the value integral.

FLP37-C.summary: Do not compare floating-point values with == or != for equality.
FLP37-C.fix: Compare with an application-appropriate epsilon tolerance.

FLP38-C.summary: Use type-generic math macros only in the ways the standard defines.
FLP38-C.fix: Pass arguments of a single real floating type.

INT30-C.summary: Ensure unsigned integer operations do not wrap unintentionally.
INT30-C.fix: Pre-check operands against limits from <stdint.h>/<limits.h> before arithmetic.

INT31-C.summary: Ensure integer conversions do not lose or misinterpret data.
INT31-C.fix: Range-check before narrowing or sign-changing conversions.

INT32-C.summary: Ensure signed integer operations do not overflow (undefined behavior).
INT32-C.fix: Guard arithmetic with explicit bound checks before the operation.

INT33-C.summary: Ensure division and remainder operations cannot divide by zero.

INT34-C.summary: Keep shift distances non-negative and below the operand's bit width.
INT34-C.fix: Validate the shift count before shifting.

INT35-C.summary: Use a type's real precision, not its sizeof-derived bit count.
INT35-C.fix: Do not assume CHAR_BIT is 8 or that padding bits are absent.

INT36-C.summary: Convert between pointers and integers only when the value is representable.
INT36-C.fix: Use intptr_t/uintptr_t if the conversion is genuinely required.

MEM30-C.summary: Do not access memory after it has been freed.
MEM30-C.fix: Null pointers immediately after free; audit ownership so each allocation has exactly one release path.

MEM31-C.summary: Free dynamically allocated memory exactly once.
MEM31-C.fix: Set pointers to NULL after free and guard release paths.

MEM33-C.summary: Allocate and copy structures with a flexible array member dynamically and correctly.
MEM33-C.fix: Size the allocation as sizeof(struct) plus the array length.

MEM34-C.summary: Only free memory that was dynamically allocated.
MEM34-C.fix: Never free stack, static, or offset pointers.

MEM35-C.summary: Allocate sufficient memory for an object, guarding size computations against overflow.
MEM35-C.fix: Validate n and check n <= SIZE_MAX / sizeof(elem) before multiplying in allocation sizes.

MEM36-C.summary: Do not assume realloc preserves an over-aligned object's alignment.
MEM36-C.fix: Allocate a fresh aligned block and copy instead.

MSC30-C.summary: Do not rely on rand() for pseudorandom numbers.
MSC30-C.fix: Use a platform CSPRNG where randomness quality matters.

MSC32-C.summary: Seed pseudorandom generators properly; do not use rand() where quality matters.
MSC32-C.fix: Use a CSPRNG for anything security-relevant.

MSC33-C.summary: Do not pass invalid data to asctime()/similar fixed-buffer time functions.
MSC33-C.fix: Use strftime with a sized buffer.

MSC37-C.summary: Ensure control never falls off the end of a value-returning function.
MSC37-C.fix: Return a value explicitly on every path, including the final one.

MSC38-C.summary: Do not take the address of a predefined identifier that may be a macro.
MSC38-C.fix: Call it directly; assert and errno may not be real objects.

MSC39-C.summary: Do not call va_arg() on a va_list whose value is indeterminate (already consumed by another traversal, or used after va_end).
MSC39-C.fix: Bracket each traversal with va_start/va_end; take a va_copy before an independent second pass; never va_arg after va_end.

MSC40-C.summary: Do not violate the language's documented constraints.
MSC40-C.fix: Fix the constraint violation rather than relying on a compiler extension.

MSC41-C.summary: Never embed secrets or credentials in source code.
MSC41-C.fix: Load secrets from provisioned storage at run time; source is not a vault.

POS30-C.summary: Use readlink() correctly, including its non-terminating result.
POS30-C.fix: readlink does not null-terminate; terminate the buffer yourself.

POS34-C.summary: Do not pass putenv() a pointer into automatic storage.
POS34-C.fix: Pass a pointer to static or heap storage that outlives the environment.

POS35-C.summary: Avoid races when checking whether a path is a symbolic link.
POS35-C.fix: Open with O_NOFOLLOW and inspect the descriptor rather than the path.

POS36-C.summary: Drop privileges in the correct order when relinquishing them.
POS36-C.fix: Drop supplementary groups, then the group, then the user.

POS37-C.summary: Verify that privilege relinquishment actually succeeded.
POS37-C.fix: Check the return value, and re-verify by attempting to regain them.

POS38-C.summary: Beware descriptor-sharing races between parent and child after fork.
POS38-C.fix: Close descriptors the child must not inherit, or use O_CLOEXEC.

POS39-C.summary: Apply correct byte ordering when moving data between systems.
POS39-C.fix: Convert with the htons/ntohl family at every boundary.

POS44-C.summary: Do not terminate threads by sending signals.
POS44-C.fix: Signal a cooperative shutdown flag and let the thread return.

POS47-C.summary: Do not use asynchronously cancelable threads.
POS47-C.fix: Use deferred cancellation and explicit cancellation points.

POS48-C.summary: Do not unlock or destroy a mutex owned by another thread.
POS48-C.fix: Only the locking thread may unlock; destroy only when unheld.

POS49-C.summary: Protect multi-thread-accessed data with a mutex, and isolate adjacent data.
POS49-C.fix: Pad or align members so unrelated data does not share a cache line or bit-field unit.

POS50-C.summary: Give objects shared between pthreads adequate storage duration.
POS50-C.fix: Do not pass a stack address to a thread that may outlive the frame.

POS51-C.summary: Lock pthread mutexes in a predefined order to avoid deadlock.
POS51-C.fix: Establish and document a global lock ordering.

POS52-C.summary: Do not perform blocking operations while holding a POSIX lock.
POS52-C.fix: Release the lock before any blocking I/O or wait.

POS53-C.summary: Use only one mutex for concurrent waits on a given condition variable.
POS53-C.fix: Bind each condition variable to exactly one mutex.

POS54-C.summary: Check and handle errors returned by POSIX library calls.
POS54-C.fix: Test every return value and handle the failure path explicitly.

PRE30-C.summary: Do not build a universal character name by pasting tokens together.
PRE30-C.fix: Write the \u escape directly rather than assembling it with ##.

PRE31-C.summary: Avoid side effects in arguments to unsafe (multi-evaluating) macros.
PRE31-C.fix: Evaluate arguments into locals before invoking the macro, or convert the macro to a function.

PRE32-C.summary: Do not place preprocessor directives inside the argument list of a function-like macro.
PRE32-C.fix: Move the #if/#endif outside the macro invocation.

SIG30-C.summary: Call only async-signal-safe functions inside signal handlers.
SIG30-C.fix: Set a volatile sig_atomic_t flag in the handler; do work in the main loop.

SIG31-C.summary: Do not access shared objects from inside a signal handler.
SIG31-C.fix: Restrict handlers to writing a volatile sig_atomic_t flag.

SIG34-C.summary: Do not re-register a handler with signal() from inside an interruptible handler.
SIG34-C.fix: Use sigaction with the appropriate flags instead.

SIG35-C.summary: Do not return normally from a handler for a computational exception.
SIG35-C.fix: Terminate, or longjmp to a recovery point, instead of returning.

STR30-C.fix: Copy the literal into a writable array before modifying it.

STR31-C.summary: Guarantee destination storage is large enough for string data plus the null terminator (gets/strcpy/strcat/sprintf are unbounded).
STR31-C.fix: Use bounded APIs (strncpy_s-style, snprintf) with correct size arguments and verify truncation.

STR32-C.summary: Do not pass non-null-terminated character sequences to functions that expect strings.
STR32-C.fix: Ensure explicit null termination after bounded copies.

STR34-C.summary: Cast a char to unsigned char before widening it to a larger integer type.
STR34-C.fix: Write (unsigned char)c so a negative char does not sign-extend.

STR37-C.summary: Pass <ctype.h> functions only values representable as unsigned char.
STR37-C.fix: Cast the argument to unsigned char at the call site.

STR38-C.summary: Do not mix narrow and wide string types or their function families.
STR38-C.fix: Use the wide-character functions consistently for wchar_t data.

WIN30-C.summary: Match each allocation with the deallocation function from the same family.
WIN30-C.fix: Free with the counterpart of whatever allocated the block.
